subject: Re: contrast: AREtomo vs WARP
body:
hi, as far as I know, aretomo does local /patches alignment and meybe 
some extra stuff to get nice tomograms.

But not everything it does can be "described" in the xf. Actually, tilt 
angle images rotation remains constant for aretomo wereas for imod or 
other alignment method it may have small adjustments.

This differences may lead you to differences in tomogram reconstructions 
and later STA wit M or relion4 that use that xf.

El 23/11/2023 a las 13:36, mrJ escribió:
> dear community,
>
> I have included AREtomo as part of my WARP pipeline in order to get 
> .xf file for alignment.  The AREtomo tomograms look quite nice, 
> however when I use the .xf file to generate the WARP tomogram I notice 
> the contrast is substantially worse.  See image below.  The tomogram 
> looks otherwise reasonable, it just has really bad contrast.  For 
> AREtomo I was also using WBP (which I think WARP does).
>
> Is this to be expected? is it because WARP has corrected CTF and 
> therefore less contrast?
>
> thanks!
>
>
>
> Screenshot 2023-11-23 at 1.35.02 PM.png
